value_accrual_summary: CFX accrues value primarily through inflationary PoS staking rewards (~4% APY funded by protocol emission, not real fees) and a partial fee-burn mechanism where 50% of base transaction fees are burned and 50% go to miners/validators, meaning stakers capture diluted emission while fee burn provides modest deflationary pressure only when network usage is high.

_notes.burn_detail: CIP-107 introduced a storage-point mechanism and fee burn: 50% of base transaction fees are burned. Prior to CIP-107, fees went entirely to miners. The burn is fee-volume-dependent and not guaranteed to be deflationary against emission.
_notes.staking_detail: PoS staking rewards are funded by protocol-level token issuance (inflation), not by captured transaction fees. The ~4% figure is the target APY stated in official docs; actual rate fluctuates with total staked CFX. CIP-43 introduced the PoS chain and staking reward structure.
_notes.staking_apr_source: Official docs cite ~4% as the designed staking interest rate; live rate is NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER from CoinGecko free tier without a direct staking APR endpoint for CFX.
_notes.why_partial: Exact real-time staking APR and precise fee-burn volumes require on-chain data queries or ConfluxScan API; the ~4% figure is the protocol-design target, not a live observed rate.
